title: Stock Indexes Mixed on Higher Oil, Earnings
body: CONCLUDED Last Updated: 15 hours ago By Joseph Michael Stonor, Dow Jones Newswires European stock indexes were mixed in early trade after Brent crude oil rose back above $110 a barrel. Chemicals and healthcare stocks slip as the Europe-wide Stoxx 600 fell 0.3%. London's FTSE 100 was down 0.1%. Losses were softened by gains for oil major BP, which was up 2.8% after posting higher profits resulting from a jump in oil prices due to the U.S.-Iran war. Barclays slid 3% after the bank's fixed-income traders missed expectations. The German DAX was down 0.2%. Pharmaceutical group Novartis slid 4.4% after reporting lower profits, dragging on the healthcare sector more widely. France's CAC 40 was 0.3% lower as basic materials stocks slip.
